title: Michigan Court Rules (MCR)
description: Complete listing of all Michigan Court Rules, organized by chapter and rule number. Promulgated by the Michigan Supreme Court and governing practice and procedure in all Michigan courts.

rules.title: Title
rules.summary: Establishes the title 'Michigan Court Rules' and the citation format.

rules.title: Purpose and Construction
rules.summary: Rules are to be construed to secure the just, speedy, and economical determination of every action.

rules.title: Applicability of Rules
rules.summary: Defines which courts and proceedings the rules apply to, including all Michigan trial courts.

rules.title: Rules of Construction
rules.summary: Rules of statutory construction applicable to the Michigan Court Rules.

rules.title: Definitions
rules.summary: Defines terms used throughout the rules, including 'action,' 'court,' 'judge,' and 'party.'

rules.title: Forms
rules.summary: Authorizes and recognizes SCAO-approved forms; compliance with approved forms constitutes compliance with the rules.

rules.title: Signatures
rules.summary: Every pleading, motion, or document must be signed by the attorney or unrepresented party.

rules.title: Computation of Time
rules.summary: Governs how time periods are computed, including counting of days, weekends, and holidays.

rules.title: Court Records Defined; Filing, Service, and Sealing of Documents
rules.summary: Defines court records, e-filing requirements, and procedures for sealing confidential documents.

rules.title: Applicability
rules.summary: Applies to all civil actions in circuit court and other courts of record unless otherwise specified.

rules.title: Persons Who May Sue or Be Sued
rules.summary: Governs the capacity of individuals, corporations, partnerships, and other entities to be parties.

rules.title: Disqualification of Judge
rules.summary: Grounds and procedure for disqualifying a judge from hearing a case, including financial interest and prior involvement.

rules.title: Incarcerated Parties
rules.summary: Special procedures for parties who are incarcerated, including service and attendance at hearings.

subchapters.title: Commencement of Action; Service of Process; Pleadings; Motions

rules.title: Forms of Action
rules.summary: Civil actions are commenced by filing a complaint. Michigan has a single form of civil action.

rules.title: Summons; Expiration of Summons; Dismissal for Lack of Progress
rules.summary: Requirements for summons issuance, 91-day validity, and dismissal for failure to serve.

rules.title: Process; Who May Serve
rules.summary: Identifies who may serve process, including sheriffs, court officers, and private persons.

rules.title: Proof of Service
rules.summary: Requirements for filing proof of service, including affidavit of service content.

rules.title: Manner of Service of Process
rules.summary: Methods of serving process on individuals, corporations, partnerships, governmental entities, and minors.

rules.title: Notice by Posting or Publication
rules.summary: When and how service by publication or posting may be used as an alternative to personal service.

rules.title: Service and Filing of Pleadings and Other Documents
rules.summary: Rules for serving and filing documents after the complaint, including methods and proof of service.

rules.title: Time; Computation; Extension
rules.summary: Time limits for responsive pleadings (21 days for complaint; 21 days for cross-claims) and procedures for extensions.

rules.title: Security for Costs
rules.summary: Court may require a party to post security for costs when ordered.

rules.title: Pleadings Allowed; Motions
rules.summary: Lists allowed pleadings: complaint, answer, reply to counterclaim, answer to cross-claim, third-party complaint, and third-party answer.

rules.title: General Rules of Pleading
rules.summary: Requires plain statement of facts showing entitlement to relief; addresses alternative and inconsistent pleadings.

rules.title: Pleading Special Matters
rules.summary: Specific requirements for pleading fraud, mistake, negligence, conditions precedent, and official documents.

rules.title: Form of Pleadings and Other Papers
rules.summary: Format requirements: caption, party names, case number, court name, and page limitations.

rules.title: Verification; Sanctions (Superseded by MCR 1.109)
rules.summary: Formerly governed verification of pleadings; now superseded by MCR 1.109.

rules.title: Defenses and Objections; How and When Presented
rules.summary: Every defense to a claim must be asserted in the responsive pleading or by motion.

rules.title: Summary Disposition
rules.summary: Michigan's equivalent of summary judgment. Grounds include: (C)(1) lack of subject matter jurisdiction; (C)(2) lack of personal jurisdiction; (C)(5) failure to state a claim; (C)(10) no genuine issue of material fact.

rules.title: Appearances
rules.summary: Procedures for attorneys entering and withdrawing appearances, including pro hac vice admission.

rules.title: Amended and Supplemental Pleadings
rules.summary: A party may amend a pleading once as of right before a responsive pleading is served, or by leave of court.

rules.title: Motion Practice
rules.summary: Requirements for motions: must state with particularity the grounds, relief sought, and supporting brief.

rules.title: Defendant Class Actions
rules.summary: Governs class actions where defendants are certified as a class.

rules.title: Substitution of Parties
rules.summary: Procedures for substituting parties on death, incompetency, transfer of interest, or substitution of public officers.

rules.title: Signing of Pleadings
rules.summary: Signature requirement; attorney's signature constitutes certification of good faith and factual and legal basis.

rules.title: Parties Plaintiff and Defendant; Capacity
rules.summary: Who may be a party; capacity to sue and be sued; real party in interest.

rules.title: Joinder of Claims; Contingent Claims
rules.summary: A party may join all claims it has against another party.

rules.title: Compulsory and Permissive Joinder; Counterclaims and Cross-Claims
rules.summary: Counterclaims arising from the same transaction must be pleaded or are waived; cross-claims against co-parties are permissive.

rules.title: Counterclaims and Cross-Claims
rules.summary: Detailed procedures for asserting counterclaims (against opposing parties) and cross-claims (against co-parties).

rules.title: Third-Party Practice (Impleader)
rules.summary: A defendant may implead a third party who may be liable to the defendant for all or part of the plaintiff's claim.

rules.title: Class Actions
rules.summary: Requirements for class certification: numerosity, commonality, typicality, and adequacy of representation.

rules.title: Interpleader
rules.summary: Allows a person holding property claimed by multiple parties to join those parties and require them to litigate their claims.

rules.title: Intervention of Right and Permissive Intervention
rules.summary: A non-party may intervene as of right when it has a claim or defense and its interest may be impaired.

rules.title: Necessary and Indispensable Parties
rules.summary: Courts must join parties whose absence would impair complete relief or impair their interests.

rules.title: Availability and Timing of Discovery
rules.summary: Discovery is available in all civil cases; timing and scope limitations; duty to supplement.

rules.title: General Rules Governing Discovery
rules.summary: Scope of discovery: any non-privileged matter relevant to the subject matter. Protective orders. Duty to supplement.

rules.title: Depositions Before Action or Pending Appeal
rules.summary: Perpetuation of testimony before an action is filed or while an appeal is pending.

rules.title: Persons Before Whom Depositions May Be Taken
rules.summary: Officers, notaries, and persons authorized by court order who may administer oaths for depositions.

rules.title: Depositions on Oral Examination
rules.summary: Notice requirements, procedure for oral depositions, objections, and deponent conduct.

rules.title: Depositions on Written Questions
rules.summary: Alternative to oral depositions using pre-submitted written questions; cross-examination questions.

rules.title: Use of Depositions in Court Proceedings
rules.summary: When and how depositions may be used at trial or on motions, including for impeachment.

rules.title: Interrogatories to Parties
rules.summary: A party may serve up to 20 interrogatories (unless otherwise ordered). Answers due in 28 days.

rules.title: Production of Documents, Electronically Stored Information, and Tangible Things
rules.summary: Requests for production of documents and ESI; access to land and property; 28-day response deadline.

rules.title: Physical and Mental Examination of Persons
rules.summary: Court may order examination when physical or mental condition is in controversy. Report of examining physician.

rules.title: Requests for Admission
rules.summary: A party may request admissions of fact or genuineness of documents; 28-day response; effect of admission.

rules.title: Failure to Make Discovery; Sanctions
rules.summary: Court may impose sanctions including dismissal, default, contempt, or cost-shifting for discovery failures.

rules.title: Failure to Make Discovery; Sanctions (Alternate)
rules.summary: Addresses specific sanctions for failure to comply with discovery orders under MCR 2.312.

rules.title: Discovery of Medical Information
rules.summary: Special rules for discovery of medical records, authorizations, and physician testimony.

rules.title: Video Depositions
rules.summary: Authorizes depositions to be recorded by video; technical requirements and use at trial.

rules.title: Discovery from Non-Parties
rules.summary: Procedures for obtaining discovery from non-parties via subpoena and court order.

rules.title: Pretrial Procedures; Conferences; Scheduling Orders
rules.summary: Court may direct parties to appear for pretrial conference to simplify issues, amend pleadings, obtain admissions, and consider ADR.

rules.title: Use of Communication Equipment
rules.summary: Allows use of videoconferencing and telephonic equipment for hearings and depositions by court order.

rules.title: Case Evaluation (Formerly Mediation)
rules.summary: Mandatory case evaluation panel for civil cases over $50,000; sanctions for rejection of evaluation award.

rules.title: Voluntary Facilitative Mediation
rules.summary: Parties may voluntarily submit to facilitative mediation; mediator neutrality and confidentiality.

rules.title: Offers to Stipulate to Entry of Judgment
rules.summary: A party may make an offer of judgment; if rejected and the case goes to trial, the rejecting party may be liable for costs if the judgment is not more favorable than the offer.

rules.title: Video Proceedings
rules.summary: Standards for conducting court proceedings via video technology.

rules.title: Telephone and Video Proceedings
rules.summary: Court may permit testimony or appearances by telephone or video; factors to consider.

rules.title: Settlement; Dismissal; Entry of Judgment
rules.summary: Parties may settle at any time; dismissal by stipulation; consent judgment entry.

rules.title: Alternative Dispute Resolution
rules.summary: Courts may order or recommend ADR, including mediation, case evaluation, and arbitration.

rules.title: Mediation
rules.summary: Court-ordered mediation procedures, mediator qualifications, and confidentiality of mediation communications.

rules.title: Assignment of Cases for Trial
rules.summary: Procedures for placing cases on the trial calendar; priority cases; notice of trial.

rules.title: Subpoenas
rules.summary: Requirements for issuing subpoenas to compel witness attendance and document production at trial.

rules.title: Adjournments
rules.summary: Court has discretion to grant adjournments for good cause; presumption against adjournments after trial date set.

rules.title: Dismissal of Actions
rules.summary: Voluntary dismissal by plaintiff (before answer, by stipulation, or by court order); involuntary dismissal for failure to prosecute.

rules.title: Consolidation; Separate Trials
rules.summary: Court may consolidate actions with common questions; may order separate trials to avoid prejudice or promote convenience.

rules.title: Subpoena; Order to Attend
rules.summary: Subpoenas for attendance at trial; service requirements; geographic limits; sanctions for disobedience.

rules.title: Conduct of Trials
rules.summary: General trial conduct rules: opening statements, order of proof, closing arguments, and courtroom decorum.

rules.title: Jury Trial of Right
rules.summary: The right to jury trial as preserved by the Michigan Constitution; issues tried by jury vs. court.

rules.title: Trial by Jury or by Court
rules.summary: Demand for jury trial required within 28 days of service of last pleading directed to the issue.

rules.title: Impaneling the Jury
rules.summary: Jury selection procedures including jury list, examination of jurors, challenges, and oath administration.

rules.title: Impaneling the Jury
rules.summary: Seating of jurors; sequestration; jury separation during deliberations.

rules.title: Instructions to the Jury; Objections
rules.summary: Procedure for submitting, reviewing, and objecting to jury instructions before they are given.

rules.title: Conduct of Jury Trial
rules.summary: Juror notes, juror questions, juror discussions during trial, and juror access to exhibits during deliberations.

rules.title: Rendering Verdict
rules.summary: Jury deliberation procedures, verdict forms, polling the jury, and handling incomplete verdicts.

rules.title: Special Verdicts; Interrogatories
rules.summary: Court may require the jury to return a special verdict or answer written interrogatories.

rules.title: Motion for Directed Verdict
rules.summary: A party may move for directed verdict at close of opposing party's case or at close of all evidence.

rules.title: Findings by Court
rules.summary: In non-jury trials, the court must state its findings of fact and conclusions of law.

rules.title: Judgment on Partial Findings (Non-suit)
rules.summary: In non-jury trial, a party may move for judgment after opposing party's presentation.

rules.title: Judgments
rules.summary: Types of relief available including money judgment, declaratory relief, injunctive relief, and specific performance.

rules.title: Entry of Judgments and Orders
rules.summary: Procedures for preparing, submitting, and entering judgments and orders; objections to proposed judgments.

rules.title: Default and Default Judgment
rules.summary: Entry of default when a party fails to plead or defend; default judgment procedures; setting aside default.

rules.title: Judgment on Multiple Claims or Multiple Parties
rules.summary: Court may enter final judgment as to fewer than all claims or parties when appropriate.

rules.title: Declaratory Judgments
rules.summary: Courts have power to declare rights and obligations in cases of actual controversy.

rules.title: New Trials; Amendment of Judgment
rules.summary: Grounds for new trial: jury misconduct, newly discovered evidence, errors of law, inadequate or excessive damages.

rules.title: Relief from Judgment or Order
rules.summary: Grounds for relief from judgment: mistake, inadvertence, excusable neglect, newly discovered evidence, fraud, void judgment, prior judgment satisfied.

rules.title: Harmless Error; Objections; Preservation of Error
rules.summary: Error is harmless if it does not affect substantial rights. Errors must be preserved by timely objection.

rules.title: Stay of Proceedings to Enforce Judgment
rules.summary: Automatic stay upon filing of motion for new trial; stay on appeal; bond requirements.

rules.title: Disability of Judge
rules.summary: Procedures when trial judge is unable to perform post-trial duties, including substitution of another judge.

rules.title: Proceedings Subsequent to Judgment
rules.summary: Procedures for enforcing and collecting on judgments after entry.

rules.title: Receivers
rules.summary: Appointment, powers, duties, and discharge of receivers appointed by circuit courts.

rules.title: Judgment Creditor Examinations
rules.summary: Procedure for examining a judgment debtor about assets and ability to satisfy judgment.

rules.title: Taxation of Costs
rules.summary: Prevailing party entitled to costs; what costs are taxable; motion to retax costs.

rules.title: Interest on Judgments
rules.summary: Prejudgment and post-judgment interest rates and calculation under MCL 600.6013.

rules.title: Security; Surety Bonds
rules.summary: Requirements for posting bonds or other security in civil proceedings.

rules.title: Garnishment After Judgment
rules.summary: Procedure for garnishing wages, bank accounts, or other assets of a judgment debtor.

rules.title: Garnishment Before Judgment
rules.summary: Pre-judgment attachment of defendant's assets; bond requirements; defendant's rights.

rules.title: Attachment
rules.summary: Writ of attachment to seize defendant's property before judgment to secure potential judgment.

rules.title: Claim and Delivery
rules.summary: Procedure to recover specific personal property wrongfully taken or held.

rules.title: Arrest
rules.summary: Civil arrest in specific limited circumstances; writ of capias ad respondendum.

rules.title: Receivers; Execution
rules.summary: Supplemental procedures for receivers appointed in post-judgment enforcement actions.

rules.title: Applicability; Definitions
rules.summary: Applies to actions for divorce, separate maintenance, annulment, paternity, custody, parenting time, and support.

rules.title: Commencement of Action; Interim Orders
rules.summary: Complaint filing requirements; ex parte and interim orders for custody, support, and use of marital home.

rules.title: Service; Proof of Service
rules.summary: Service requirements in domestic relations cases including service on incarcerated parties.

rules.title: Personal Protection Orders
rules.summary: Procedures for obtaining PPOs in domestic violence, stalking, and sexual assault cases; ex parte relief; hearings.

rules.title: Prior and Subsequent Orders; Res Judicata
rules.summary: Effect of prior and subsequent orders affecting minors in domestic relations cases.

rules.title: Uniform Interstate Family Support Act (UIFSA)
rules.summary: Procedures for enforcing and modifying child support orders from other states under UIFSA.

rules.title: Temporary Orders
rules.summary: Standards and procedures for temporary custody, parenting time, support, and injunctive orders.

rules.title: Friend of the Court
rules.summary: Role and duties of the Friend of the Court in domestic relations cases, including investigation and recommendation.

rules.title: Domestic Relations Cases; Prosecuting Attorney
rules.summary: Prosecuting attorney's role in Title IV-D child support enforcement cases.

rules.title: Hearings and Trials
rules.summary: Trial procedures in domestic relations cases; use of testimony vs. affidavits; judicial notice of FOC reports.

rules.title: Judgment or Order
rules.summary: Requirements for domestic relations judgments including specific provisions for custody, parenting time, and support.

rules.title: Post-Judgment Transfer
rules.summary: Transfer of domestic relations cases between counties after change of domicile of parties.

rules.title: Spousal Support Agreements
rules.summary: Requirements for spousal support (alimony) provisions in divorce judgments.

rules.title: Enforcement of Domestic Relations Orders
rules.summary: Show cause, contempt, and income withholding procedures for enforcing domestic relations orders.

rules.title: Domestic Violence and Weapons
rules.summary: Procedures for gun and weapon prohibition orders in domestic violence cases.

rules.title: Domestic Relations Arbitration
rules.summary: Rules for binding arbitration of domestic relations disputes under the Domestic Relations Arbitration Act.

rules.title: Court-Ordered Mediation in Domestic Relations Cases
rules.summary: Standards for court-ordered mediation in custody and parenting time disputes.

rules.title: Change of Domicile
rules.summary: Procedures when a parent seeks to relocate a child more than 100 miles from the other parent.

rules.title: Parenting Time
rules.summary: Standards for parenting time orders, make-up parenting time, and enforcement.

rules.title: Income Withholding for Support
rules.summary: Automatic income withholding for child support in all cases; employer obligations; modification.

rules.title: Support Arrearage Judgments
rules.summary: Entry of judgment for unpaid support arrearage; collection procedures.

rules.title: Emancipation of Minor
rules.summary: Petition procedure for emancipation of a minor; court's consideration of best interests of minor.

rules.title: Adoption; Applicability
rules.summary: Scope of adoption rules; applicability to agency adoptions, step-parent adoptions, and adult adoptions.

rules.title: Petition for Adoption
rules.summary: Contents of adoption petition; required attachments including home study and background checks.

rules.title: Service of Process; Notice
rules.summary: Who must receive notice in adoption proceedings including parents, alleged fathers, and agencies.

rules.title: Termination of Parental Rights in Adoption Proceedings
rules.summary: Procedures for terminating parental rights as a step in the adoption process.

rules.title: Hearing on Adoption Petition
rules.summary: Hearing requirements; attendance of petitioner and adoptee; judicial interview of older children.

rules.title: Confidentiality of Adoption Records
rules.summary: Adoption records are sealed; procedures for accessing records; disclosure to adoptees and birth parents.

rules.title: Adoption Records; Central Registry
rules.summary: SCAO maintenance of central adoption registry; identity disclosure procedures.

rules.title: Appeals from Administrative Agencies and License Revocations
rules.summary: Procedures for circuit court review of decisions by state and local administrative agencies.

rules.title: Condemnation of Property
rules.summary: Eminent domain proceedings; jury trial on just compensation; court appointment of commissioners.

rules.title: Class Actions
rules.summary: Requirements for class certification: numerosity, commonality, typicality, and adequacy; notice to class members; settlement approval.

rules.title: Derivative Actions by Shareholders
rules.summary: Procedures for shareholder derivative suits; demand requirement; security for expenses.

rules.title: Habeas Corpus
rules.summary: Procedure for writ of habeas corpus to inquire into the cause of detention or imprisonment.

rules.title: Arbitration
rules.summary: Court proceedings to confirm, vacate, or modify arbitration awards under the Michigan Arbitration Act.

rules.title: Interpleader
rules.summary: When a stakeholder holding property claimed by multiple parties may file an interpleader action.

rules.title: Injunctions
rules.summary: Requirements for preliminary injunctions and temporary restraining orders; bond; notice; duration.

rules.title: Contempt of Court Outside the Immediate Presence of the Court
rules.summary: Indirect contempt procedures: notice, hearing, burden of proof, and sanctions.

rules.title: Appointment of Receiver for Assignment for Benefit of Creditors
rules.summary: Procedures for court-appointed receivers in insolvency and creditor protection proceedings.

rules.title: Applicability; Definitions
rules.summary: Defines terms and scope of juvenile proceedings including abuse/neglect, delinquency, and status offenses.

rules.title: General Provisions
rules.summary: Confidentiality of juvenile proceedings and records; victim rights; interpreter services.

rules.title: Definitions
rules.summary: Definitions specific to juvenile proceedings: child, court, juvenile, petition, and ward of the court.

rules.title: Preliminary Inquiry
rules.summary: Intake officer's assessment of whether a formal petition should be filed; diversion options.

rules.title: Preliminary Hearing
rules.summary: Initial hearing in child protective proceedings to determine if child should remain in home.

rules.title: Petition
rules.summary: Requirements for the petition in child abuse/neglect proceedings; authorized petitioners.

rules.title: Preliminary Hearing
rules.summary: Probable cause determination; placement decisions; parental rights at preliminary hearing.

rules.title: Bench Warrant; Taking Child into Protective Custody
rules.summary: Procedures for emergency removal of a child from parental custody without prior court order.

rules.title: Pleas of Admission or No Contest
rules.summary: Procedure for accepting parent's admission or no-contest plea in child protective proceedings.

rules.title: Trial
rules.summary: Trial procedures in child abuse/neglect cases; burden of proof (clear and convincing); jury waived.

rules.title: Dispositional Hearing
rules.summary: Hearing to determine appropriate placement and services after adjudication in child protective case.

rules.title: Review and Permanency Planning Hearings
rules.summary: Periodic review hearings; ASFA timelines for permanency; reasonable efforts findings.

rules.title: Post-Dispositional Proceedings; Modification; Termination of Court Jurisdiction
rules.summary: Modifying or terminating dispositional orders when circumstances change.

rules.title: Termination of Parental Rights
rules.summary: Grounds and procedures for terminating parental rights in child protective proceedings.

rules.title: Termination of Parental Rights
rules.summary: Statutory grounds for termination; best interests analysis; burden of proof.

rules.title: Post-Termination Review Hearings
rules.summary: Court oversight of children after parental rights are terminated until adoption or another permanency goal is achieved.

rules.title: Indian Children; Michigan Indian Family Preservation Act
rules.summary: Special procedures under ICWA and MIFPA for proceedings involving Native American children.

rules.title: Applicability
rules.summary: Chapter 4 applies to all proceedings in district court, supplemented by Chapter 2 where applicable.

rules.title: Commencement of Civil Action
rules.summary: Civil actions in district court are commenced by filing a complaint and paying the filing fee.

rules.title: Service of Process in District Court
rules.summary: Methods for serving process in district court civil actions; personal, substituted, and mail service.

rules.title: Civil Infraction Actions
rules.summary: Procedures for civil traffic and non-traffic infraction cases; informal hearings; formal hearings; default.

rules.title: Summary Proceedings to Recover Possession of Premises (Eviction)
rules.summary: Expedited procedures for landlord-tenant eviction cases; notice requirements; default judgment; stay.

rules.title: Summary Proceedings; Land Contract Forfeiture
rules.summary: Procedures for forfeiture of land contracts; notice requirements; redemption rights.

rules.title: Small Claims Actions
rules.summary: Informal procedure for claims up to $7,000 ($10,000 for sole proprietors); no attorneys; simplified evidence rules.

rules.title: Small Claims; Removal and Transfer
rules.summary: Defendant may remove a small claims case to the general civil docket for a more formal proceeding.

rules.title: Small Claims; Judgment and Costs
rules.summary: Judgment entry in small claims; costs recoverable; judgment lien on real property.

rules.title: Criminal Procedure in District Court
rules.summary: General criminal procedure rules applicable in district court, supplemented by Chapter 6.

rules.title: Arraignment in District Court
rules.summary: Arraignment on a misdemeanor charge; reading of charge; entry of plea; setting of bail.

rules.title: Misdemeanor Trial
rules.summary: Trial procedures for misdemeanor cases in district court; jury of 6; verdict requirements.

rules.title: Sentencing in District Court
rules.summary: Sentencing procedures, pre-sentence investigations, and post-sentence rights in district court.

rules.title: Applicability; Definitions
rules.summary: Applies to all probate proceedings; defines 'fiduciary,' 'interested person,' 'minor,' and 'petition.'

rules.title: Waiver and Consent
rules.summary: Interested persons may waive notice or consent to relief sought; effect of waiver on jurisdiction.

rules.title: Waiver of Fees
rules.summary: Procedure for waiving court fees for indigent parties in probate proceedings.

rules.title: Commencement of Proceedings
rules.summary: All probate proceedings are commenced by filing a verified petition; contents of petition.

rules.title: Original Petition; Notice; Interested Persons
rules.summary: Notice requirements when a petition is filed; who must receive notice; method and timing of notice.

rules.title: Interested Persons
rules.summary: Defines who are interested persons in various types of proceedings; changes in interested persons.

rules.title: Service of Process and Notice in Probate Proceedings
rules.summary: Service of process and notice requirements; personal service, mail, and publication.

rules.title: Filing and Service
rules.summary: Filing requirements for documents in probate proceedings; electronic filing.

rules.title: Hearings
rules.summary: Setting and conducting hearings; waiver of hearing; contested hearings; evidence.

rules.title: Presence at Hearing
rules.summary: Who must be present at hearing; attendance of the ward or protected individual; use of videoconferencing.

rules.title: Continuances
rules.summary: Standards for granting continuances in probate proceedings.

rules.title: Entry of Orders
rules.summary: Procedures for preparing and entering orders in probate court.

rules.title: Record
rules.summary: Court records in probate proceedings; public access; confidential records.

rules.title: Appeals from Probate Court
rules.summary: Procedures for appealing probate court orders to the Court of Appeals.

rules.title: Enforcement of Orders
rules.summary: Contempt and other enforcement procedures for probate court orders.

rules.title: Accounts; Accountings
rules.summary: Requirements for annual and final accountings by fiduciaries (personal representatives, guardians, conservators).

rules.title: Final Account and Petition for Settlement
rules.summary: Procedures for closing a probate estate; final accounting; distribution; discharge of personal representative.

rules.title: Settlement of Accounts
rules.summary: Informal and formal procedures for approving fiduciary accounts.

rules.title: Bond
rules.summary: When a fiduciary must post bond; amount; surety requirements; waiver.

rules.title: Inventory
rules.summary: Requirements for personal representative's inventory of estate assets; filing and notice.

rules.title: Appointment and Qualification of Fiduciary
rules.summary: Procedures for appointing personal representatives, guardians, and conservators.

rules.title: Removal and Resignation of Fiduciary
rules.summary: Grounds and procedures for removing a fiduciary or accepting resignation; successor appointment.

rules.title: Supervised Administration of Decedent's Estates
rules.summary: Procedures for formal, court-supervised administration (DA cases).

rules.title: Guardianship of Minor
rules.summary: Petition requirements; notice; standards for appointing guardian for a minor; annual reports.

rules.title: Guardianship of an Incapacitated Individual
rules.summary: Procedures for appointing and monitoring guardians for incapacitated adults; limited guardianship.

rules.title: Conservatorship
rules.summary: Procedures for appointing and monitoring conservators to manage financial affairs of protected individuals.

rules.title: Standby and Successor Guardianship
rules.summary: Procedures for designating and activating standby and successor guardians.

rules.title: Protective Orders (Financial)
rules.summary: Procedures for obtaining protective orders to manage financial affairs without full conservatorship.

rules.title: Decedent's Estates; Applicability
rules.summary: Scope of estate rules; applicability to testate and intestate estates.

rules.title: Testacy Proceedings; Determination of Heirs
rules.summary: Formal proceedings to admit will to probate or establish intestacy; determination of heirs.

rules.title: Small Estates; Affidavit of Heirship
rules.summary: Summary procedures for estates below the statutory threshold; affidavit of heirship in lieu of probate.

rules.title: Supervised Administration
rules.summary: Formal supervised estate administration (DA) requiring court approval at each stage.

rules.title: Determination of Heirs; Succession Without Administration
rules.summary: Procedures for determining heirs without full estate administration.

rules.title: Claims Against Estate
rules.summary: Notice to creditors; time limits for filing claims; procedures for allowing or disallowing claims.

rules.title: Personal Representative
rules.summary: Duties, powers, and limitations of personal representatives; appointment and qualification.

rules.title: Information to Devisees and Heirs
rules.summary: Personal representative's obligation to inform beneficiaries and heirs of the estate.

rules.title: Closing of Decedent's Estate
rules.summary: Procedures for closing an estate; statement of completion; discharge of personal representative.

rules.title: Applicability; Definitions
rules.summary: Applies to all criminal proceedings in Michigan courts; defines offense levels (felony, misdemeanor, civil infraction).

rules.title: Purpose and Scope
rules.summary: Rules are to be construed to secure just, speedy, and economical determination of every criminal proceeding.

rules.title: Electronic Filing and Service
rules.summary: Requirements for electronic filing of criminal documents in courts with e-filing systems.

rules.title: Speedy Trial
rules.summary: Defendant's constitutional and statutory right to a speedy trial; procedures to assert the right; consequences of violation.

rules.title: Right to Assistance of Lawyer; Advice; Appointment
rules.summary: Defendant's Sixth Amendment right to counsel; procedures for appointment of counsel at public expense.

rules.title: Video and Audio Proceedings
rules.summary: Standards for conducting arraignments, probable cause conferences, and other hearings by video.

rules.title: Confidential Records
rules.summary: Sealing of criminal records in appropriate cases; access to confidential records.

rules.title: Victim's Rights
rules.summary: Crime victim's rights in criminal proceedings including notice, attendance, and statement at sentencing.

rules.title: Complaint
rules.summary: Requirements for criminal complaint; sworn statement of facts; magistrate review for probable cause.

rules.title: Arrest Without Warrant; Arraignment on Complaint
rules.summary: Procedures following warrantless arrest; probable cause determination; arraignment within 24 hours.

rules.title: Arraignment on the Warrant or Summons
rules.summary: Arraignment procedures for defendant arrested on warrant or served with summons to appear.

rules.title: Arraignment in the Circuit Court
rules.summary: Arraignment on the information in circuit court after bind-over from district court.

rules.title: Probable Cause Conference
rules.summary: Mandatory conference within 7-14 days of arraignment to discuss plea, case evaluation, and scheduling.

rules.title: Release or Detention Pending Trial
rules.summary: Bail and bond standards; conditions of pretrial release; detention for dangerous defendants.

rules.title: Competency to Stand Trial
rules.summary: Procedures when defendant's competency to stand trial is in question; evaluation; hearing; commitment.

rules.title: Transfer of Case from District Court to Circuit Court
rules.summary: Procedures for transfer (bind-over) following preliminary examination.

rules.title: Preliminary Examination
rules.summary: Defendant's right to preliminary examination within 14 days of arraignment; probable cause standard.

rules.title: Waiver of Preliminary Examination
rules.summary: Defendant may waive preliminary examination and be bound over directly to circuit court.

rules.title: The Indictment or Information
rules.summary: Requirements for the information (formal criminal charge): party names, offense, MCL citation, date, signature.

rules.title: Arraignment on the Information
rules.summary: Formal arraignment in circuit court; entry of plea; scheduling; waiver of reading of information.

rules.title: Discovery
rules.summary: Criminal discovery rights: prosecution must disclose witness lists, statements, reports, and exculpatory evidence.

rules.title: Expert Witnesses
rules.summary: Disclosure of expert witness identity and opinions in criminal cases; procedures for challenging experts.

rules.title: Available Pleas
rules.summary: A defendant may plead: not guilty, guilty, nolo contendere, not guilty by reason of insanity, or guilty but mentally ill.

rules.title: Plea of Guilty or Nolo Contendere
rules.summary: Requirements for accepting a plea: understanding of rights, voluntariness, factual basis, and sentencing consequences.

rules.title: Plea of Insanity; Guilty But Mentally Ill
rules.summary: Procedures for entering special pleas of not guilty by reason of insanity or guilty but mentally ill.

rules.title: Plea of Guilty to Lesser Offense or Included Offense
rules.summary: Plea to lesser-included offense or reduced charge with prosecution's concurrence.

rules.title: Withdrawal and Nullification of Plea
rules.summary: Standards for allowing plea withdrawal before sentencing; after sentencing only to correct a manifest injustice.

rules.title: Plea Agreement Disclosure
rules.summary: Prosecutor and defense must disclose plea agreement terms on the record in open court.

rules.title: Right to Trial by Jury
rules.summary: Defendant in a felony case has a right to trial by jury of 12; misdemeanors with more than 92 days incarceration—jury of 6.

rules.title: Waiver of Jury Trial
rules.summary: Defendant may waive jury trial with approval of the court; waiver must be in writing or on the record.

rules.title: Nonjury Trial
rules.summary: Procedures for bench trial in criminal cases; findings of fact and conclusions of law required.

rules.title: Preliminary Matters Before Trial
rules.summary: Motions in limine; suppression motions; matters to be resolved before trial commences.

rules.title: Selection of Jurors
rules.summary: Jury selection procedures: questionnaires, voir dire, challenges for cause, peremptory challenges.

rules.title: Alternate Jurors
rules.summary: Selection and use of alternate jurors; when alternates are seated in deliberations.

rules.title: Conduct of Jury Trial
rules.summary: General conduct of criminal jury trial from opening statements through verdict.

rules.title: Peremptory Challenges
rules.summary: Number of peremptory challenges in criminal cases (felony: 12 each; misdemeanor: 5 each).

rules.title: Challenge for Cause
rules.summary: Grounds and procedure for challenges for cause including bias, relationship, and inability to be fair.

rules.title: Motion for Directed Verdict of Acquittal
rules.summary: Defense may move for directed verdict at end of prosecution's case; court's standard of review.

rules.title: Acquittal After Submission to Jury
rules.summary: Post-verdict motion for acquittal notwithstanding verdict; standard.

rules.title: Sentencing; Appointment of Appellate Counsel
rules.summary: Pre-sentence investigation report; sentencing guidelines; scoring; victim impact statement; right to allocution; appointment of appellate counsel.

rules.title: Judgment of Conviction
rules.summary: Requirements for written judgment of conviction; entry; copies to parties and MDOC.

rules.title: Correction of Sentence
rules.summary: Court may correct an invalid sentence at any time; procedure for motion to correct sentence.

rules.title: Modification of Sentence
rules.summary: Circumstances under which a sentence may be modified; Cobbs evaluation; sentence bargain.

rules.title: Custodial Credit
rules.summary: Defendant entitled to credit for time spent in custody prior to sentencing.

rules.title: New Trial
rules.summary: Grounds and procedures for motion for new trial in criminal cases; newly discovered evidence.

rules.title: Motions Affecting Judgment
rules.summary: Post-trial motions: new trial, judgment notwithstanding verdict, and arrest of judgment.

rules.title: Disability of Judge
rules.summary: Procedures when the trial judge is unable to continue; substitution of judge for sentencing or new trial.

rules.title: Probation
rules.summary: Conditions of probation; modification; revocation procedures; violation hearings.

rules.title: Scope; Purpose
rules.summary: Post-conviction relief is available to challenge a conviction or sentence after direct appeal.

rules.title: Motion for Relief from Judgment
rules.summary: Post-conviction motion; must be filed in trial court that imposed sentence; time limits.

rules.title: Contents of Motion
rules.summary: What the motion must contain: grounds for relief, facts, law, affidavits, and argument.

rules.title: Response to Motion
rules.summary: Prosecution's response; defendant's reply; expansion of record.

rules.title: Expansion of Record
rules.summary: Court may allow submission of materials outside the existing record including affidavits and documents.

rules.title: Evidentiary Hearing
rules.summary: When the court must hold an evidentiary hearing; defendant's right to be present.

rules.title: Decision
rules.summary: Court must state its findings and conclusions of law; entry of order.

rules.title: Entitlement to Relief
rules.summary: Standard for granting post-conviction relief; showing of cause and prejudice required for procedurally defaulted claims.

rules.title: Successive Motions
rules.summary: Second and successive post-conviction motions are disfavored; must show good cause for not raising issue earlier.

rules.title: Applicability; Definitions
rules.summary: Applies to all appeals and original proceedings in the Court of Appeals and Supreme Court.

rules.title: Jurisdiction of Appellate Courts
rules.summary: Defines the jurisdictional bases for the Court of Appeals and Supreme Court, including appeal of right and leave to appeal.

rules.title: Calendar Cases in Appellate Courts
rules.summary: Priority scheduling for certain categories of cases (e.g., termination of parental rights, criminal appeals).

rules.title: Motions in Appellate Courts
rules.summary: General procedure for motions in the Court of Appeals and Supreme Court.

rules.title: Filing and Service
rules.summary: Requirements for filing documents in appellate courts; electronic filing; proof of service.

rules.title: Record on Appeal
rules.summary: What constitutes the appellate record; correction or supplementation of record.

rules.title: Confidential Materials
rules.summary: Procedures for protecting confidential materials in appellate proceedings.

rules.title: Original Proceedings; Extraordinary Writs
rules.summary: Standards and procedures for original writs (mandamus, prohibition, superintending control) in appellate courts.

rules.title: Definitions
rules.summary: Definitions for Chapter 7.100: 'appeal,' 'appellant,' 'appellee,' 'circuit court,' and 'lower court.'

rules.title: Claim of Appeal to Circuit Court
rules.summary: Filing a claim of appeal from district court or probate court to circuit court; deadline (21 days); filing fee.

rules.title: Cross-Appeal
rules.summary: Time and procedure for filing a cross-appeal.

rules.title: Stay of Proceedings
rules.summary: Standards and procedure for staying lower court proceedings or judgment pending appeal.

rules.title: Filing and Service
rules.summary: Filing documents in circuit court appeals; proof of service; transcript orders.

rules.title: Record on Appeal
rules.summary: Contents of the record on appeal from district or probate court to circuit court.

rules.title: Briefs in Circuit Court Appeals
rules.summary: Brief format, content, length, and filing deadlines in circuit court appeals.

rules.title: Oral Argument in Circuit Court Appeals
rules.summary: Scheduling and conduct of oral argument in circuit court appeals.

rules.title: Decision; Motion for Reconsideration
rules.summary: Circuit court's decision on appeal; motion for reconsideration standard and deadline.

rules.title: Costs in Circuit Court Appeals
rules.summary: Recovery of costs by prevailing party on appeal.

rules.title: Miscellaneous Relief in Circuit Court Appeals
rules.summary: Other relief available in circuit court appeals including remand and supplementing the record.

rules.title: Organization and Operation
rules.summary: Court of Appeals sits in panels of 3 judges; districts; assignment of cases; en banc proceedings.

rules.title: Definitions
rules.summary: Definitions for Chapter 7.200: 'appeal of right,' 'application for leave,' 'final order,' and 'interlocutory order.'

rules.title: Jurisdiction of the Court of Appeals
rules.summary: Final orders are appealable of right; interlocutory orders require leave; list of specifically appealable orders.

rules.title: Filing Appeal of Right; Appearance
rules.summary: Deadline to file claim of appeal of right (21 days from entry of final order); entry fee; docketing statement.

rules.title: Application for Leave to Appeal
rules.summary: Procedure for seeking leave to appeal interlocutory orders; grounds; answer; standard for granting.

rules.title: Original Proceedings; Superintending Control
rules.summary: Procedure for original writs in the Court of Appeals including superintending control over lower courts.

rules.title: Cross-Appeal
rules.summary: Appellee may file a cross-appeal within 21 days after service of appellant's claim of appeal.

rules.title: Stays Pending Appeal
rules.summary: Standards for staying enforcement of a trial court judgment while appeal is pending.

rules.title: Bond; Other Security
rules.summary: When appellant must post bond or other security to obtain a stay pending appeal.

rules.title: Record on Appeal
rules.summary: Contents of the record on appeal to the Court of Appeals; duty to order transcripts; correction of record.

rules.title: Motions in Court of Appeals
rules.summary: Procedure for filing motions in the Court of Appeals; emergency motions; single-judge orders.

rules.title: Briefs
rules.summary: Brief content, format (Times New Roman 14-point or Courier 12-point), page limits (50 pages), and deadlines in COA.

rules.title: Calendar Cases; Submission
rules.summary: Scheduling of cases for submission on briefs or oral argument; priority docket.

rules.title: Oral Argument
rules.summary: When oral argument is held; each side gets 30 minutes unless otherwise ordered.

rules.title: Opinions, Orders, Judgments, and Final Process
rules.summary: Publication of COA opinions; binding precedent; unpublished opinions; effective date of judgment.

rules.title: Miscellaneous Relief
rules.summary: COA may grant any just order, including remand, reversal, and dismissal.

rules.title: Involuntary Dismissal
rules.summary: COA may dismiss appeal for failure to prosecute or comply with rules.

rules.title: Vexatious Proceedings
rules.summary: Sanctions including fees and costs for filing frivolous appeals.

rules.title: Costs and Fees
rules.summary: Recovery of costs in Court of Appeals; taxable costs; attorney fees as sanction.

rules.title: Organization and Operation
rules.summary: Supreme Court consists of 7 Justices; Chief Justice; quorum; concurrence of 4 required for decision.

rules.title: Applications for Leave to Appeal
rules.summary: Procedure to seek MSC review; grounds for granting leave; time limit (56 days from COA decision).

rules.title: Original Proceedings in Supreme Court
rules.summary: Procedure for original writs in the Supreme Court including superintending control.

rules.title: Cross-Appeals in Supreme Court
rules.summary: Appellee may file cross-appeal within 28 days of service of appellant's application.

rules.title: Stays Pending Appeal to Supreme Court
rules.summary: Procedures for stays of COA or trial court judgments pending Supreme Court review.

rules.title: Record on Appeal in Supreme Court
rules.summary: Record on appeal to MSC; when transmission is required; additions to record.

rules.title: Motions in Supreme Court
rules.summary: Procedure for filing motions in the Supreme Court; single justice orders.

rules.title: Briefs and Appendices in Supreme Court
rules.summary: Brief requirements in MSC: format, length, content, and filing deadlines after leave is granted.

rules.title: Oral Argument in Supreme Court
rules.summary: Oral argument in Supreme Court; each side gets 30 minutes unless otherwise ordered.

rules.title: Opinions, Orders, and Judgments of Supreme Court
rules.summary: Publication and precedential status of MSC opinions; reconsideration procedures.

rules.title: Miscellaneous Relief
rules.summary: Supreme Court may grant any just order including remand, reversal, and modification.

rules.title: Vexatious Proceedings
rules.summary: Sanctions for frivolous applications and vexatious conduct in Supreme Court proceedings.

rules.title: Trial Court Case File Management
rules.summary: Requirements for managing and retaining trial court files; electronic case files; SCAO oversight.

rules.title: Conducting Court Business Outside the County
rules.summary: When and how circuit or probate court may hold court outside its county.

rules.title: Court Hearing Equipment
rules.summary: Standards for audio and video equipment used in courtrooms.

rules.title: Electronic Media and Photography
rules.summary: Rules governing cameras, recording devices, and electronic media in court proceedings.

rules.title: Recording of Court Proceedings
rules.summary: Verbatim recording of all court proceedings; court reporter or electronic recording; transcription.

rules.title: Remote and Hybrid Proceedings
rules.summary: Standards for conducting court proceedings remotely or in hybrid (in-person and remote) format.

rules.title: Assignment of District Court and Probate Court Judges
rules.summary: Assignment of visiting judges to district and probate courts to cover absences and heavy caseloads.

rules.title: Jury Certificates
rules.summary: Issuance of jury certificates to jurors; compensation rates.

rules.title: Payments and Deposits into Court
rules.summary: Rules governing deposits of money or property into court custody.

rules.title: Court Reporting
rules.summary: Certification and duties of court reporters; official reporters; freelance reporters; transcript fees.

rules.title: Attorney Reporting Requirements
rules.summary: Annual reporting obligations of attorneys to the State Bar; address changes; inactive status.

rules.title: Records Management and Retention
rules.summary: Retention schedules for court records; destruction of records; transfer to state archives.

rules.title: Facsimile and Electronic Transmission
rules.summary: Standards for accepting documents filed by facsimile or electronic transmission.

rules.title: Court Interpreters and Translators
rules.summary: Appointment and compensation of qualified interpreters for Limited English Proficient parties and witnesses.

rules.title: Chief Judge; Duties and Powers
rules.summary: Selection of chief judge; administrative authority over the trial court; caseload management.

rules.title: Assignment of Cases
rules.summary: Random assignment of cases; reassignment; recusal procedures; presiding judge for all purposes.

rules.title: Local Court Rules
rules.summary: Courts may adopt local rules not inconsistent with the MCR; submission to SCAO for approval.

rules.title: Law Students and Recent Graduates
rules.summary: Supervised practice by law students and recent graduates under a supervising attorney in court.

rules.title: Processing of Court Fines and Costs
rules.summary: Collection and disbursement of fines, costs, and assessments imposed by courts.

rules.title: Temporary Absence of Judge
rules.summary: Procedures for designating a substitute judge during a judge's temporary absence.

rules.title: Photographing, Filming, and Broadcasting
rules.summary: Standards for media coverage of court proceedings; courtroom decorum; judge's discretion.

rules.title: Court Filing Standards
rules.summary: Standards for all documents filed in Michigan courts including format, type size, and margins.

rules.title: Court Records; Access and Redaction
rules.summary: Public access to court records; categories of confidential records; redaction of personal identifiers.

rules.title: Court Facilities
rules.summary: Standards for adequate court facilities; SCAO oversight; minimum requirements.

rules.title: Reporting Requirements for Trial Courts
rules.summary: Statistical reporting obligations of trial courts to SCAO; caseload reports; financial reports.

rules.title: Annual Statistical Report
rules.summary: Annual caseload statistics to be reported by all courts to SCAO by January 31.

rules.title: Fiscal Year Financial Report
rules.summary: Courts' obligation to report annual financial data to SCAO; fee revenue; costs.

rules.title: Public Access to Court Proceedings
rules.summary: Presumptive right of public access to court proceedings and records; closure requires specific findings.

rules.title: Limited English Proficient Persons
rules.summary: Courts' obligations to provide meaningful access to LEP persons; interpreter services; translated forms.

rules.title: Commission on Judicial Tenure; Authority
rules.summary: Authority and structure of the Judicial Tenure Commission; constitutional basis.

rules.title: Definitions
rules.summary: Definitions: 'judge,' 'commission,' 'formal complaint,' 'master,' and 'supreme court.'

rules.title: Organization of Commission
rules.summary: JTC membership; chair; quorum; staff; budget.

rules.title: Confidentiality of Proceedings
rules.summary: JTC proceedings are confidential until formal complaint is filed; exceptions.

rules.title: Grounds for Discipline of Judges
rules.summary: Grounds: misconduct in office, failure to perform duties, mental or physical disability, conviction of felony.

rules.title: Investigative Subpoenas
rules.summary: JTC may issue subpoenas during investigations; witness immunity; non-compliance.

rules.title: Consent Agreements; Interim Suspension
rules.summary: JTC may negotiate consent agreements; emergency interim suspension for serious misconduct.

rules.title: Appointment of Counsel for Judge
rules.summary: Judge's right to retained or appointed counsel in JTC proceedings.

rules.title: Complaint and Investigation
rules.summary: Anyone may file a complaint with JTC; investigation procedures; dismissal; exoneration.

rules.title: Appointment of Master
rules.summary: JTC may appoint a master to conduct hearings and take evidence.

rules.title: Review by Commission
rules.summary: After master's report, commission reviews evidence and recommends discipline to Supreme Court.

rules.title: Review by Supreme Court
rules.summary: Supreme Court reviews JTC recommendations; may impose, modify, or reject recommended discipline.

rules.title: Confidentiality
rules.summary: Proceedings remain confidential until formal complaint is filed; records of dismissed complaints sealed.

rules.title: Costs
rules.summary: Supreme Court may assess costs against a disciplined judge.

rules.title: Reinstatement
rules.summary: Procedures for a disciplined or suspended judge to seek reinstatement.

rules.title: Public Nature of Formal Proceedings
rules.summary: Once formal complaint is filed, proceedings are public; notice to parties.

rules.title: Contempt of Commission
rules.summary: JTC may seek contempt citation from Supreme Court for disobedience of subpoenas.

rules.title: Duties of Clerk of Supreme Court
rules.summary: Clerk's responsibilities regarding JTC matters filed with the Supreme Court.

rules.title: Discipline Based on Conviction
rules.summary: Automatic interim suspension upon conviction of a felony or crime involving dishonesty.

rules.title: Resignation
rules.summary: Effect of judge's resignation on JTC proceedings.

rules.title: Discipline Based on Action in Another Jurisdiction
rules.summary: JTC may impose discipline based on discipline imposed in another jurisdiction.

rules.title: Interim Suspension on Felony Conviction
rules.summary: Supreme Court may immediately suspend a judge convicted of a felony pending JTC proceedings.

rules.title: Applicability
rules.summary: Applies to all disciplinary proceedings against Michigan-licensed attorneys.

rules.title: Definitions
rules.summary: Defines 'attorney,' 'grievance administrator,' 'commission,' 'board,' and 'hearing panel.'

rules.title: Attorney Grievance Commission
rules.summary: AGC organization, authority, and procedures; master rule for attorney discipline intake.

rules.title: Attorney Discipline Board
rules.summary: ADB organization; review of hearing panel decisions; authority to impose, affirm, or modify discipline.

rules.title: Hearing Panels
rules.summary: Three-person panels (2 lawyers, 1 layperson) that conduct evidentiary hearings in attorney discipline cases.

rules.title: Confidentiality
rules.summary: Discipline proceedings are confidential until formal complaint is filed; exceptions for public safety.

rules.title: Grounds for Discipline
rules.summary: Grounds: violation of MRPC, criminal conviction, conduct prejudicial to administration of justice.

rules.title: Attorney Discipline; Authority
rules.summary: Types of discipline: disbarment, suspension, reprimand, admonishment, probation, restitution.

rules.title: Filing of Complaints
rules.summary: Anyone may file a grievance against an attorney; filing procedures; initial review by AGC.

rules.title: Investigation
rules.summary: AGC investigates grievances; power to issue investigative subpoenas; interview witnesses.

rules.title: Disclosure of Investigation
rules.summary: When and how the AGC must disclose investigation to the respondent attorney.

rules.title: Investigation File; Dismissal; Admonishment
rules.summary: Dismissal of frivolous grievances; informal admonishment; escalation to formal complaint.

rules.title: Formal Complaint
rules.summary: When and how a formal complaint is filed; contents; respondent's obligation to answer.

rules.title: Service of Formal Complaint
rules.summary: Service of formal complaint on respondent; consequences of failure to respond.

rules.title: Answer to Formal Complaint
rules.summary: Respondent's answer requirements; admission and denial; affirmative defenses.

rules.title: Supplemental and Amended Complaints
rules.summary: Procedures for amending or supplementing a formal complaint.

rules.title: Prehearing Conferences
rules.summary: Prehearing conference to simplify issues, obtain stipulations, and schedule hearing.

rules.title: Hearing Procedure
rules.summary: Conduct of evidentiary hearing before hearing panel; rules of evidence apply; burden of proof (preponderance).

rules.title: Evidence
rules.summary: Evidence rules applicable in discipline hearings; admissibility; documentary evidence; testimony.

rules.title: Decision of Hearing Panel
rules.summary: Hearing panel's written decision; findings of fact; conclusions of law; recommended discipline.

rules.title: Appeal to Board
rules.summary: Either party may appeal hearing panel decision to ADB within 21 days.

rules.title: Review by Board
rules.summary: ADB standard of review; may increase, decrease, or affirm panel discipline.

rules.title: Review by Supreme Court
rules.summary: Final review by MSC; Supreme Court has ultimate authority over attorney discipline.

rules.title: Costs
rules.summary: Disciplined attorney may be ordered to pay costs of proceedings.

rules.title: Restitution
rules.summary: Disciplined attorney may be ordered to pay restitution to aggrieved clients.

rules.title: Reinstatement
rules.summary: Procedures for seeking reinstatement after suspension or disbarment; showing of rehabilitation.

rules.title: Contempt
rules.summary: Non-compliance with subpoenas or orders may result in contempt proceedings.

rules.title: Reciprocal Discipline
rules.summary: Discipline imposed in another jurisdiction triggers reciprocal discipline proceedings in Michigan.

rules.title: Consent Order of Discipline
rules.summary: Respondent attorney may consent to a specified discipline; procedure for approval.

rules.title: Interim Suspension
rules.summary: AGC may seek emergency interim suspension when attorney poses immediate danger to clients or public.

rules.title: Reciprocal Discipline; Grounds
rules.summary: Types of out-of-state discipline that trigger Michigan reciprocal proceedings.

rules.title: Summary Proceedings
rules.summary: Expedited procedures for discipline based on conviction of a crime or another jurisdiction's discipline.

rules.title: Disbarment on Consent
rules.summary: An attorney may consent to disbarment in lieu of contested discipline proceedings.

rules.title: Suspension for Failure to Pay Child Support
rules.summary: Attorney's license may be suspended for failure to comply with a child support order.

rules.title: Suspension for Default on Student Loan
rules.summary: Attorney's license may be suspended for defaulting on a state-guaranteed student loan.

rules.title: Discipline Based on Conviction of Crime
rules.summary: Procedures when an attorney is convicted of a crime, including felonies and misdemeanors involving moral turpitude.
